name: everme
description: Persistent memory recall for Codex sessions via EverMe MCP — pairs with the everme MCP server configured by evercli to load user profile and search prior conversations across sessions.

interface.shortDescription: Persistent cross-session memory recall for Codex
interface.longDescription: EverMe gives Codex read access to your persistent memory across sessions. The skill instructs Codex when to read the `mem://profile` and `mem://search` MCP resources exposed by the everme MCP server (configured by `evercli plugin install codex`). Use it to load user profile + relevant context at the start of a session and to recall prior conversations on demand. Saving new memories from inside Codex is not part of this plugin in the current Codex App — write back via Claude Code, Cursor, or the EverMe web UI. No model output is sent to EverMe servers unless the LLM explicitly reads a mem:// resource.
defaultPrompt: Load my EverMe memory for this Codex session.
Recall what we decided about EverMe last time.
